channel.title: Awesome Search KG
channel.description: Last 10 notes on Awesome Search KG

item.title: Doug Turnbull
item.description: Doug Turnbull Co-founder of OpenSource Connections (OSC); co-author of Relevant Search (O’Reilly).

item.title: Bonsai
item.description: Bonsai Bonsai (bonsai.io) is a managed OpenSearch / Elasticsearch hosting provider.

item.title: Autocomplete
item.description: Autocomplete Definition Autocomplete (also: autosuggest, search suggestions, typeahead) shows predicted query completions or related queries as the user types, before they submit the full search.

item.title: How to Really Design Search for a Database
item.description: How to Really Design Search for a Database A prescriptive Bonsai tutorial by Max Irwin on building search over a relational database.

item.title: How to Really Do Autocomplete
item.description: How to Really Do Autocomplete Part 1 of Max Irwin’s Bonsai autocomplete series.

item.title: Query Sampling for Relevancy Testing
item.description: Query Sampling for Relevancy Testing A Bonsai piece by Nick Zadrozny arguing that relevance testing should not choose between frequent and infrequent queries — it should sample across all frequency tiers, because each tier exposes a different class of product problem.

item.title: Search Kōans
item.description: Search Kōans A short, reflective Bonsai piece by Max Irwin distilling ~15 years of search engineering into Zen-style kōans.

item.title: What is Elasticsearch and Why You Should Use It
item.description: What is Elasticsearch and Why You Should Use It An introductory Bonsai explainer by Leo Schuster: what Elasticsearch is, its architecture, and when it earns a place in a developer’s toolkit.

item.title: What is OpenSearch and Why You Should Use It
item.description: What is OpenSearch and Why You Should Use It An introductory Bonsai explainer by Leo Schuster on OpenSearch — what it is, why it exists, and when to choose it.

item.title: Bonsai - Designing Search for a Relational Database
item.description: Bonsai — Designing Search for a Relational Database How Bonsai (via Max Irwin) makes every record type in a relational database universally findable by collapsing many tables into a single, denormalized Elasticsearch/OpenSearch index designed around user information needs rather than database struct...
